description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a prominent, curved wooden exterior and a large, convex glass screen. The front features a black bezel framing the screen, with two large rotary dials on the right side for channel and volume control, and a small label reading 'Television.' The top of the unit has two metal V-shaped antenna rods extending upward, characteristic of mid-20th-century TV designs. The sides are vented with vertical slats for heat dissipation, and the back panel includes a power switch, a socket, and additional ventilation. The overall design is boxy and robust, with rounded edges and a rich, dark wood grain finish. This television would typically be found in living rooms, garages, or even on sidewalks as discarded electronics in urban environments.
description: A vintage, wood-encased television with a convex glass screen, rotary dials, and metal antenna rods. The design features a black bezel, vented sides, and a robust, retro aesthetic.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the wooden casing, two metal antenna rods, and a back panel with a power switch and socket.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the vertical ventilation slats and the depth of the wooden enclosure.
description_view_2: The front view displays the convex glass screen, black bezel, two rotary dials, and the 'Television' label.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the vented slats and the curvature of the wooden body.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel, rectangular, with rounded corners and a matte finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two rotary dials on the front right, power switch on the back panel
